target.name: Atom Bodies
target.founder: Tyler Hayes, Doug Satzger, Eric Monsef

methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series A → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.0x) to $2M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 6 same-sector + same/adjacent-stage peers in Biotech & Health. Range: $100M - $3.3B.

methods.name: Public-Comp Anchoring
methods.skip_reason: target revenue not on file (private companies rarely disclose)
methods.rationale: Public Biotech & Health P/S median is 6.1x across 3 tickers. Cannot apply without target's revenue.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Biotech & Health

Public-co frontier-tech comp in Biotech & Health

data.narrative:
Atom Bodies is a Series A biotech company founded by Tyler Hayes, Doug Satzger, and Eric Monsef, with $2M+ in total raised. The valuation analysis draws on a peer universe of eight private and three public companies in Biotech & Health, including Series A and B stage comparables such as Eikon Therapeutics ($3.3B), Xaira Therapeutics ($2.7B), and Profluent ($600M).

Two valuation methods proved applicable. Stage-Progression anchors to the $2M raised as a proxy for current value and applies a standard 2.0x step-up multiplier typical for advancement from Series A to subsequent rounds, implying $4M. Peer-Median analysis references six same-sector, same/adjacent-stage comparables with a median valuation of $1.3B (range: $100M to $3.3B). Public-Comp Anchoring could not be applied due to the absence of revenue data, though the three public tickers in the Biotech & Health sector (RXRX, TEM, ONT.L) show a median P/S multiple of 6.1x.

The triangulated analysis yields a range of $3M (low) to $1.5B (high), with a median of $652M. The wide spread reflects the substantial variance inherent in early-stage biotech valuations and the early-stage nature of the target relative to later-stage peers. Key uncertainties include the absence of revenue, patent estate detail, and clinical/development stage specificity, all of which would meaningfully narrow the valuation range.
